execution_results.previous_definition:
create or replace view V_DAILY_SALES(
	ORDER_DATE,
	DAILY_TOTAL,
	RUNNING_TOTAL
) as
SELECT
    o.order_date,
    SUM(o.order_total)                                                      AS daily_total,
    SUM(SUM(o.order_total)) OVER (
        ORDER BY o.order_date
        ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    )                                                                       AS running_total
FROM OPT_LAB_CLONE_4.RETAIL.orders AS o
GROUP BY
    o.order_date;
execution_results.executed_sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW OPT_LAB_CLONE_4.RETAIL.V_DAILY_SALES AS
SELECT
    d1.order_date,
    SUM(d1.order_total) AS daily_total,
    SUM(d1.order_total) OVER (
        ORDER BY d1.order_date
        ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    ) AS running_total
FROM OPT_LAB_CLONE_4.RETAIL.orders AS d1
GROUP BY d1.order_date;
execution_results.error: SQL compilation error: [D1.ORDER_TOTAL] is not a valid group by expression
execution_results.message: Execution FAILED in APPLY mode. The optimized VIEW definition uses a window function directly on the base column D1.ORDER_TOTAL while also aggregating with SUM(D1.ORDER_TOTAL) and grouping only by D1.ORDER_DATE. To be valid, the window function should be applied over the aggregated daily_total (e.g., SUM(d1.order_total) OVER (...) in a subquery) rather than over the raw column in the same grouped query. Please adjust the definition accordingly (for example, aggregate in a subquery and then apply the window function) and re-run the update.
